python3 << 'EOF'
import re

# Read the file
with open('/workspace/repo/completions.go', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Add FilterHidden field to CompletionOptions struct
old_code = """\t// HiddenDefaultCmd makes the default 'completion' command hidden
\tHiddenDefaultCmd bool
}"""

new_code = """\t// HiddenDefaultCmd makes the default 'completion' command hidden
\tHiddenDefaultCmd bool
\t// FilterHidden prevents Cobra from providing completions that start with a
\t// dot (.) to the shell.  This provides a consistent way to hide internal or
\t// configuration-related entries from shell tab-completion.
\tFilterHidden bool
}"""

content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)

# Write the file back
with open('/workspace/repo/completions.go', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("Added FilterHidden field to CompletionOptions")
EOF

Looking at the code:
1. Line 483: Early return for flag completion when DisableFlagParsing==false
2. Line 544: Early return when ValidArgs are specified (before ValidArgsFunction)
3. Line 570: Final return after all completions have been gathered

The most comprehensive approach would be to apply the filter at the final return (line 570), as this is where all completions have been collected. For the early returns:
- Line 483: This is for flag completion when DisableFlagParsing==false. These flags are generated by Cobra, so they shouldn't start with a dot unless explicitly added. I think we can skip filtering here or apply it for consistency.
- Line 544: This is when ValidArgs are specified but before ValidArgsFunction. Again, these are manually specified valid args, not necessarily generated completions.

For simplicity and consistency, I'll apply the filter only at the final return (line 570). However, to be more thorough, let me check what the early returns contain:

Actually, thinking about this more - the task says to suppress "completion suggestions that start with a dot". The completions can come from:
1. Subcommands (line 511-516)
2. ValidArgs (line 530-534)
3. ArgAliases (line 538-542)
4. ValidArgsFunction (line 565-568)
5. Flag names (line 451-458)

All of these could potentially have entries starting with a dot. So I should apply the filter to all of them. But for simplicity, I'll apply it only at the final return in the main path. For the early returns (lines 483 and 544), let me also add the filter for consistency.

Let me modify the getCompletions function to apply the filter at the appropriate places.

Now let me modify the `getCompletions` function to apply the filter. I'll need to update the return statements:

python3 << 'EOF'
# Read the file
with open('/workspace/repo/completions.go', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Replace the final return statement to apply the filter
old_final_return = '\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil\n}\n\nfunc helpOrVersionFlagPresent'
new_final_return = '''\tif getFilterHidden(finalCmd) {
\t\tcompletions = filterHiddenCompletions(completions)
\t}
\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
}

func helpOrVersionFlagPresent'''

content = content.replace(old_final_return, new_final_return)

# Also apply filter to the early return for flag completions (around line 483)
old_flag_return = '\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil\n\t}\n\t} else {'
new_flag_return = '''\t\tif getFilterHidden(finalCmd) {
\t\t\tcompletions = filterHiddenCompletions(completions)
\t\t}
\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
\t}
\t} else {'''

content = content.replace(old_flag_return, new_flag_return)

# Also apply filter to the ValidArgs return (around line 544)
old_validargs_return = '\t\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Let the logic continue so as to add any ValidArgsFunction completions'
new_validargs_return = '''\t\t\tif getFilterHidden(finalCmd) {
\t\t\t\tcompletions = filterHiddenCompletions(completions)
\t\t\t}
\t\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
\t\t\t}

\t\t\t// Let the logic continue so as to add any ValidArgsFunction completions'''

content = content.replace(old_validargs_return, new_validargs_return)

# Write the file back
with open('/workspace/repo/completions.go', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("Modified getCompletions to apply filter")
EOF

\t\t\t\tcompletions = filterHiddenCompletions(completions)
\t\t\t}
\t\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil'''

new_text = '''\t\t\t\t// If there are ValidArgs specified (even if they don't match), we stop completion.
\t\t\t\t// Only one of ValidArgs or ValidArgsFunction can be used for a single command.
\t\t\t\tif getFilterHidden(finalCmd) {
\t\t\t\t\tcompletions = filterHiddenCompletions(completions)
\t\t\t}
\t\t\treturn finalCmd, completions, directive, nil'''

if old_text in content:
    content = content.replace(old_text, new_text)
    print("Fixed indentation")
else:
    print("Could not find text to fix")

# Write the file back
with open('/workspace/repo/completions.go', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF
